projects.title: Steampunk Catculus — HackNJIT
projects.description: What if you could answer math questions with your hands? Steampunk Catculus is an educational game that uses real-time hand gesture recognition to make learning math interactive and immersive. Players answer AI-generated questions through finger gestures to progress through a steampunk world.
highlights: Computer vision input via MediaPipe and OpenCV — no keyboard required
AI-generated math questions via Cloudflare for dynamic gameplay
Full game environment built with Python and Pygame
Designed and shipped end-to-end at a hackathon
projects.why: I wanted to explore what happens when you remove the barrier between a learner and the content — no clicking, no typing, just showing your work with your hands.
projects.role: Co-developer — computer vision integration, game logic, UI/UX
projects.challenge: Getting real-time gesture recognition to feel responsive and natural inside a game loop without lag tanking the experience.

projects.title: Fashion Faeries — DivHacks 2024
projects.description: 1st Place, Best in Fashion Track · Best Use of MongoDB (MLH). An award-winning web app that generates personalized outfit recommendations from user-uploaded wardrobes using machine learning and Pinterest-style inspiration — with a sustainability twist.
highlights: ML-powered outfit recommendations from user wardrobe uploads
Second-hand price comparison, resale options, and waste-reduction features
Personalized data storage and retrieval with MongoDB
Won Best in Fashion Track and Best Use of MongoDB at DivHacks 2024
projects.why: Fashion is one of the most wasteful industries on the planet. I wanted to build something that makes sustainable choices feel effortless and stylish.
projects.role: Full-stack developer — ML integration, MongoDB, frontend
projects.challenge: Delivering a polished, working product across ML, database, and UI layers in a single hackathon day.

projects.title: Eco-Tourism — DevFest
projects.description: A Svelte web app that helps travelers discover lesser-known sustainable destinations worldwide. Features mapping, eco-tour booking, and real-time incident reporting to protect fragile environments.
highlights: Built with SvelteKit for a fast, modern user experience
Destination search, eco-tour browsing, and sustainable travel tips
Real-time incident reporting to flag environmental threats
Clean, intuitive UI designed for ease of exploration
projects.why: Most travel apps optimize for popularity. I wanted one that optimizes for impact — helping people find places that need thoughtful visitors, not more crowds.
projects.challenge: Making sustainability feel like a feature people actually want, not a lecture.

projects.title: CheckMD — HackNJIT
projects.description: A surgical error prevention web app built to reduce preventable mistakes in the operating room. Streamlit-powered with Python, enhanced with HTML and CSS, and integrated with MS Excel for data management.
highlights: Critical safety checklists to reduce OR errors
Built with Streamlit and Python, styled with HTML/CSS
MS Excel integration for efficient data tracking
Presented live to hackathon judges
projects.why: Preventable surgical errors kill thousands of people every year. If a checklist app can catch even one mistake, it's worth building.
projects.role: Developer — backend logic, data integration, frontend styling
projects.challenge: Translating a high-stakes medical workflow into a clean, usable interface under time pressure.

projects.title: FTC POWERPLAY Robot — Team 14212 Metrobotics
projects.description: Programmed a competition robot that advanced to the FIRST Tech Challenge World Championship. Implemented computer vision for autonomous zone detection and full tele-op control with smooth, precise movement.
highlights: OpenCV pipeline detecting RGB values across three autonomous zones
Scored 1+5 during autonomous period
Roadrunner/odometry for velocity and acceleration control
Competed at FTC World Championship
projects.why: Robotics is where code meets the physical world and has to actually work. No hiding behind a browser refresh.
projects.role: Programmer — autonomous pipeline, tele-op control, odometry
projects.challenge: Getting autonomous zone detection reliable enough to trust in a championship match with no second chances.
